content.claim: A replication study across 75 countries and 20 years of PISA/PIRLS/TIMSS data found that research on tracking's effect on social inequality has been inconclusive; while some studies found tracking perpetuated inequality, most observed no tracking effect on social achievement gaps.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Contradicts Hanushek & Woessmann findings, indicating methodological sensitivity
Does not resolve whether early age-of-selection per se drives outcomes or institutional context (choice, funding, curriculum harmonization) does
Aggregate finding masks variation by outcome type and country context
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
